steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: "To this day" signals that the action continues into the present — it is not completed in the past or hypothetical.

steps.title: Choose the verb tense
steps.content:
Rule: "to this day" is a present-time adverbial that requires a present-tense verb.
  • Choice A — "would have been": past conditional perfect — implies the action is hypothetical or contrary to fact, contradicting "to this day". ✗
  • Choice B — "were": simple past or past subjunctive — marks a completed or hypothetical past state, not a present ongoing use. ✗
  • Choice C — "were being": past progressive — describes an ongoing action in the past only, not extending to the present. ✗
  • Choice D — "are": simple present — correctly expresses that these emulsifiers are still in use now, matching "to this day". ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
